item.title: Want to predict central banker behaviour? Look to their birth date
item.description: Formative experiences shape our views on future inflation as much as the data

item.title: My five lessons from judging this year’s International Booker Prize
item.description: Reading 128 books in eight months was a privilege — and a lasting education in making time, embracing difference and building literary muscle

item.title: Securitisation could help plug Europe’s funding gap
item.description: Deeper capital markets are essential to the continent’s growth

item.title: How Brexit made Britain a European country
item.description: With its political chaos and high-spending state, the UK is more continental than it was a decade ago

item.title: Japan fails to grapple with past in Shōwa spectacle
item.description: The celebrations were enthusiastic but the country seems to have a hard time calibrating its relationship with what went before

item.title: Blair’s diagnosis of Labour is correct
item.description: Party’s commitment to ambitious pledges before election narrowed its room for manoeuvre

item.title: AI’s mixed economic signals
item.description: Plus, the euro convergence trade

item.title: Halifax and TSB have outlived their brand-name usefulness
item.description: Bank consolidation in Britain now makes sense in targeting customers for multiple products

item.title: How did Jay Powell do at the Fed?
item.description: The outgoing chair has made some mistakes, but his decision to stand up to Trump was heroic

item.title: The surprising power of simple predictions
item.description: Experts can be sidetracked by elaborate ideas, overlooking the direct approach

item.title: The pizza oven start-up that came through the fire
item.description: Co-founders of Scotland’s Ooni faced a post-pandemic crisis after invention went global

item.title: How some blockchains enable front-running
item.description: Regulators need to tackle the issue and address the cost to investors of excessive transparency

item.title: Can GLP-1 drugs save the mall?
item.description: The advent of diet-driven shopping comes at a crucial time for many retailers

item.title: For failing water utilities, nationalisation is not a dirty word
item.description: It is easier to fund maintenance and infrastructure upgrades when freed from the temptation to pay big dividends

item.title: US and Iran: the art of the possible
item.description: Stopgap agreement leaves key issues to be decided in later talks

item.title: BP is deeply dysfunctional, but it could be worse
item.description: Investors should be concerned by Manifold’s ousting, but better to fire too many executives than too few

item.title: The steroid Olympics fell short of its own finishing line
item.description: Doping alone will not guarantee triumph in sport

item.title: Today’s windfall is a warning for state oil companies
item.description: Continuing to invest as if yesterday’s assumptions will govern tomorrow’s world means they won’t be around in 40 years

item.title: Putin and Trump don’t have the cards
item.description: The wars Russia and America have waged in Ukraine and Iran are case studies in geopolitical self-harm

item.title: Stocks’ unshakeable optimism
item.description: The bright side has been the only place to be

item.title: SpaceX to drive a Cybertruck through corporate governance norms
item.description: Provisions in the IPO prospectus will give Elon Musk more freedom — and potentially less accountability

item.title: AI stories aren’t inevitably ‘not art’
item.description: Exercising our own judgment when it comes to quality is something we should not outsource to machines

item.title: Three ways a new Labour leader could change the politics of growth
item.description: The UK needs to confront the policies that have made us poorer

item.title: There are more important things to consider when moving countries than tax
item.description: Relocating to a low-tax jurisdiction often involves unforeseen legal, emotional and social costs

item.title: The predatory advance of surveillance pricing
item.description: Extracting the maximum possible surplus by using personalised data should not be legal
